architecture.summary: PAG is a unified multi-turn reinforcement learning framework where a single LLM alternates between policy (reasoner) and verifier roles. In each turn, the model generates a reasoning attempt (policy), then generates a verification step to detect errors (generative verifier). If the verification detects an error, the model revises its answer; otherwise, it retains the original. This selective revision mechanism operates within a reinforcement learning loop that jointly trains reasoning and verification capabilities without requiring separate verifier models or multi-stage pipelines.
trained_components: LLM verifier network (error detection via generative verification)

biblio.title: PAG: Multi-Turn Reinforced LLM Self-Correction with Policy as Generative Verifier

claims.statement: PAG introduces a selective revision mechanism: the model revises its answer only when its own generative verification step detects an error, distinct from prior approaches that always generate a second attempt regardless of model confidence
The verify-then-revise workflow alleviates model collapse while jointly enhancing both reasoning and verification abilities
As a policy, PAG enhances direct generation and self-correction accuracy; as a verifier, its self-verification outperforms self-consistency

PAG is a simple and effective framework that empowers LLMs to self-correct by alternating between policy and verifier roles within a unified multi-turn reinforcement learning paradigm without requiring separate verifier models or multi-stage self-correction training pipelines
